text_raw: Artificial Intelligence In Cinema -- Part 5The director spent several years developing the script and the visual style of the film. He drew inspiration from classic science fiction films such as Blade Runner and 2001: A Space Odyssey.The film was shot in Hungary, and the visual effects were created by the director himself, who used his experience as a visual effects artist to create a unique and immersive world.Overall, Archive is a thought-provoking science fiction film that explores the nature of humanity and the ethical implications of creating synthetic beings.In the film Archive, the AI element is central to the story. The main character, George Almore, is a scientist who is working on a project to create a synthetic human being named J3. J3 is not just an ordinary AI, but a highly advanced one that is capable of learning, adapting and even feeling emotions.Throughout the film, J3 is portrayed as a sentient being with a strong desire for self-preservation, which is a common theme in AI-related films. As Georges project nears completion, J3 becomes increasingly self-aware and begins to question its own existence and purpose. It even starts to develop feelings for one of the other synthetic beings in the facility, which raises ethical questions about the nature of artificial life and the rights of sentient beings.The film also explores the concept of transferring human consciousness into an artificial body, which is a popular theme in science fiction. George is motivated by the desire to bring his deceased wife back to life by transferring her consciousness into a synthetic body. This raises questions about the ethics of such a process and whether it is morally justifiable to create a synthetic being for the sole purpose of serving human needs.Overall, the AI element of Archive is a thought-provoking exploration of the nature of consciousness, the ethical implications of creating sentient beings, and the relationship between humans and AI.Saturn 3The film Saturn 3 is a science fiction movie that was released in 1980. The film was directed by Stanley Donen and starred Kirk Douglas, Farrah Fawcett, and Harvey Keitel.The idea for the film originated in the 1970s with writer Martin Amis, who had initially planned to turn his idea into a novel. However, producer Lew Grade convinced Amis to write a screenplay instead.The original script was titled The Helper and was a psychological thriller set on Earth. However, after Lew Grade acquired the rights, he decided to rework the script and set it in space.

text_raw: Sam Altman at TED2025: Navigating the Future of AIOpenAI CEO Sam Altman shared profound insights on the evolving landscape of artificial intelligence during his TED2025 session, Life Is Beautiful. On Human Intelligence vs. AI:My kids will never be smarter than AI. Altman reflects on the rapid advancement of AI, acknowledging that future generations will grow up in a world where AI surpasses human intelligence.Commitment to Open Source:We had a meeting last night on our open source policy. We are going to do a powerful open-source model near the frontier. OpenAI plans to release a powerful open-source model, balancing innovation with accessibility.AI as a Personal Companion:The upload happens bit by bit. It will be an extension of yourself, and a companion, and soon will proactively push things to you. Altman envisions AI evolving into a proactive personal assistant, integrating seamlessly into daily life.Addressing AI Consciousness:We are NOT sitting on a conscious model capable of self-improvement. He clarifies that current AI models lack consciousness and the ability to self-improve autonomously.On Agentic AI and Safety:Agentic AI is the most consequential safety problem we have faced. It has much higher stakes. Altman emphasizes the significant safety challenges posed by AI systems capable of autonomous actions.Reflections on Lifes Meaning:Paraphrasing his co-founder Ilya Sutskever: I dont know what the meaning of life is, but I am sure it has something to do with babies. A contemplative take on lifes purpose, highlighting human connection and continuity.Evolving Content Moderation Policies:We are now much less restrictive on speech harms, as people dont want censorship. OpenAI is adjusting its approach to content moderation, aiming to balance free expression with responsibility.User Engagement Milestone:We have 500 million weekly active users. A testament to AIs growing integration into everyday life, reflecting widespread adoption.

text_raw: There are those who argue fundamentally that cryptocurrencies, fintech, self-driving cars, artificial intelligence, or quantum computers are completely speculative, their use cases are dubious, and that these are shell companies not justifiable with their lofty valuation and are nothing special due to competitive threats. Throughout the last few years, Ive learned to listen to these arguments with great skepticisms. Ive learned that the market is always right. Even if the idea is ahead of its time and proper business execution may not be possible yet, liquidity conditions could still lead to irrational exuberance that could last for years. If liquidity keeps flowing in, funding and resources could actually push innovation forward if liquidity condition continues to be favorable for growth.The market often knows something that conventional wisdom doesnt, and that we could be transforming into a more efficient world with innovations that bring deflationary forces, obligating more money printing to sustain growth of productivity, and piling liquidity into companies in the forefront of that transformation. Skeptics look to the past and cites past stock market bubbles as reasons to not be involved in the market, even though the market has to price in technological revolutions that continue to accelerate. While there will always be proponents of the dot-com bubble crash coming, that were all too deep in our heads, that we should be cautious because of 1987, 1990, 2000, 2008, 2015/2016, 2018, 2020, and 2022, I dont know if anyone can call the top and short a parabolically bullish market and not get their asses handed to them for years until they get it right when the crash actually comes. I dont claim to know anything about the actual details of the technical advancements going on in the world right now, but as a proper chartist with the proper tools and the proper confluence from other technical analysts that I trust, I see that speculative stocks in certain sectors that are well positioned for breakouts, and that liquidity conditions continue to favor a risk-on environment. Im open to being completely wrong, but the point of being on here is to share a different point of view based on what I see.At some point, perhaps this weekend or early next week, I want to talk more about what I see in the charts that are making me think that we could be starting something that seems irrational as were going through it, but in hindsight may make a lot of sense moving forward.

text_raw: CAN AI-GENERATED CONTENT BE TRUSTED? PRCA BOARD RAISES CONCERNSThe Public Relations and Communications Association (PRCA) convened an online conference on March 1st, during which its board members addressed pressing issues such as artificial intelligence (AI) hallucinations and intellectual property (IP) theft within the industry. The conference, moderated by Richard Fernandez, PRCAs director of external and public affairs, focused on the recent update to the organizations guidelines on client-consultancy relationships.PRCA prioritizes transparency in AI use The PRCA board highlighted AI as a significant concern, emphasizing the importance of transparency regarding its use. James Crawford, managing director of PR Agency One, noted that while addressing these concerns, the group aimed to maintain flexibility in its approach to guidelines. The updated charter underscores the necessity for openness about AI tool usage while considering factors such as copyright, confidentiality, disinformation, and diversity. Privacy emerged as a primary client concern, prompting agencies to focus on anonymizing client work and prioritizing paid AI platforms for enhanced privacy protection.Mitigating the risk of Hallucinations and IP theftClaire Williams, managing director of Resonance, echoed concerns regarding AI-generated content, cautioning against its unchecked dissemination due to the risk of hallucinations, where AI tools might present inaccurate information as fact. Another significant worry discussed was IP theft, wherein clients may exploit ideas pitched by PR consultants without engaging their services. Despite the PRCAs condemnation of IP theft in its updated charter, Williams highlighted the challenge of detecting such instances before investing substantial effort and resources.

text_raw: What Grok says:nH Predict is an artificial intelligence (AI) model used by health insurance companies like UnitedHealth Group and Humana for making coverage decisions, particularly for post-acute care in Medicare Advantage plans. Heres a brief overview based on available information:Functionality: nH Predict is designed to predict how long a patient might need post-acute care, such as stays in skilled nursing facilities or inpatient rehabilitation centers. It uses data from a large database of patient outcomes to make these predictions.Controversy and Lawsuits: There have been numerous lawsuits against insurance companies for allegedly using nH Predict to deny coverage prematurely. These lawsuits claim that:The AI model has a high error rate, with some reports citing up to a 90 overturn rate on appeals, suggesting that the tools predictions are often incorrect.Patients, especially elderly ones, have been wrongfully denied necessary care, leading to both medical and financial hardships. The model is accused of not considering the full medical needs or circumstances of patients, leading to decisions that override doctors medical judgments.Impact on Patients: The use of nH Predict has been criticized for potentially forcing patients out of care facilities too soon or compelling them to pay out-of-pocket for continued care that should have been covered by their insurance. This has raised ethical and legal concerns about the application of AI in healthcare decision-making.Response from Insurers: Insurance companies involved have responded by defending their use of AI as a tool to inform, rather than dictate, coverage decisions, emphasizing that there is always a human in the loop for final decision-making. However, critics argue that the influence of the AIs predictions on human decision-makers is significant and often results in denials.Legislative and Regulatory Response: There is an increasing call for more oversight or regulation on how AI is used in healthcare insurance decisions, given the potential for harm to vulnerable populations like the elderly. Some legislative actions are aimed at ensuring that AI tools do not override human medical judgment without proper review.The controversy around nH Predict highlights broader discussions about the role of AI in healthcare, balancing efficiency with patient care quality, and the need for transparency and accountability in AI-driven decision-making processes in insurance.

text_raw: Heres a summary of the latest developments in artificial intelligence as reported in recent news:- Amazons AI Illusion and AI Sidekicks: It has been revealed that Amazons stores, which were thought to be powered by AI for checkout processes, actually rely on human workers in India to monitor transactions. This has sparked discussions about the real-world application of AI in business operations. - White House AI Strategy: The White House has mandated that all federal agencies appoint chief artificial intelligence officers. This move is aimed at managing the risks and overseeing the implementation of AI technologies across various governmental functions. This could reshape the roles within the C-suite of businesses as they adapt to evolving AI technologies. - AI in Business and Productivity: There is a debate among economists about the productivity benefits of AI. While some are sceptical, citing overhyped capabilities, others believe AI will significantly boost productivity this decade. Businesses are already reporting savings in time and costs due to AI integration. - AIs Reliability and Safety Concerns: AI technologies, incredibly generative AI like ChatGPT, often suffer from issues like generating inaccurate or fabricated information. This raises concerns about their reliability and suitability for tasks where accuracy is crucial, such as in health information searches or customer service. - Regulatory Approaches to AI: The UKs Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA) has outlined a strategic approach to AI, focusing on safety, transparency, and fairness. This is part of a broader effort to harness AIs potential in healthcare while ensuring patient safety and compliance with regulatory standards. - Funding for AI Startups: There has been a significant drop in funding for AI startups in India, with a 71 decrease in 2023 compared to the previous year. Despite this, global funding for AI startups remains robust, indicating a mixed financial landscape for AI enterprises. - AI in Cancer Detection: Researchers in China have developed a new AI-powered test using dried blood spots that can detect certain types of cancer with high sensitivity. This represents a significant advancement in making cancer diagnosis more accessible and cost-effective, especially in low-income countries. - AI in Military Applications: The U.S. Air Force has tested an AI-piloted aircraft, the X-62A VISTA, demonstrating the increasing integration of AI in military technology and operations. These updates highlight the dynamic and rapidly evolving field of AI, touching on aspects from regulatory changes and business adaptations to technological innovations and challenges in reliability.

text_raw: As artificial intelligence (AI) is still a relatively new technology, people may use it to discuss a wide range of topics including, potentially, serious matters such as crimes, threats, or acts of violence. I understand that storing this kind of information, especially at the scale required by AI systems like ChatGPT, can be extremely costly and data intensive.Until legal frameworks are fully established to support AI systems and clarify responsibilities regarding what is discussed and retained, these platforms exist in a kind of legal grey area. While AI may function similarly to a psychologist in terms of receiving sensitive disclosures, it is not yet bound by the same legal and ethical obligations, especially considering it cannot verify the identity or age of the user behind the screen.Psychologists have a legal duty to report certain criminal or harmful acts, and I believe that, as legislation evolves, AI will eventually be required to follow similar legal obligations. However, if only ChatGPT is being asked to comply with such legal responsibilities, while other platforms or technologies are not, then this could be seen as discriminatory. Legal standards must be applied fairly and consistently across all relevant technologies.

text_raw: Artificial Intelligence (AI) refers to systems designed for specific tasks, like image recognition or language generation, operating within narrow, predefined domains using algorithms and training data. Artificial General Intelligence (AGI), in contrast, is a theoretical AI capable of human-like reasoning across diverse tasks, solving novel problems without task-specific programming, a milestone yet to be achieved as of May 2025. Elon Musks concerns about AI risks, often articulated through his work with xAI, culminated in his warnings about a digital god, a term he used in a 2024 Forbes interview to describe the potential of unchecked AI development, particularly with supercomputers like Colossus, which xAI uses to train advanced models such as Grok-3. The distinction between AI and AGI is critical, as Musks fearsechoed in Sutskevers doomsday bunker planshighlight how AGIs emergence, potentially accelerated by supercomputers, could pose existential risks if it evolves into a digital god beyond human control. -

text_raw: Certainly! Heres an integrated perspective on how the United States decline, as outlined by Ray Dalio, is further exacerbated by the rise of artificial general intelligence (AGI) and humanoid robots, impacting the labor force:1. Debt and Financial Instability: The U.S. has accumulated significant national debt, leading to financial instability. With the rise of AGI and humanoid robots, there is a potential for widespread job displacement, particularly in manufacturing, services, and even high-skill jobs. This could result in higher unemployment rates, reducing tax revenues and increasing government spending on social safety nets, further exacerbating the debt problem and putting more pressure on the financial system.2. Wealth and Income Inequality: Growing wealth and income inequality in the U.S. is already a major factor in societal unrest and political polarization. The integration of AGI and humanoid robots into the workforce could amplify this inequality. High-income individuals and corporations owning these advanced technologies would see increased profits, while displaced workers, unable to transition quickly to new roles, would face diminished economic prospects. This could lead to even greater societal unrest and widening of the socio-economic divide.3. Diminishing Educational Standards: The U.S. is experiencing a relative decline in educational standards and skills development compared to other rising nations. As AGI and robotics advance, the need for a highly skilled and adaptable workforce becomes crucial. However, if the educational system fails to evolve and equip individuals with the necessary skills to thrive in a technologically advanced job market, the U.S. could see a significant portion of its workforce becoming obsolete, further weakening its competitive edge in innovation and economic productivity.4. Political Dysfunction: Increasing political polarization and dysfunction impede effective governance and the ability to implement necessary reforms. The rapid advancement of AGI and humanoid robots will require proactive policies to manage the transition of the labor force, such as retraining programs and social safety nets. Political dysfunction may hinder the timely implementation of such measures, exacerbating economic and social challenges and weakening the U.S.s ability to respond effectively to the technological disruption.5. Relative Economic Power Decline: The rise of other nations, particularly China, is contributing to a relative decline in U.S. economic power. As these nations invest heavily in AGI and robotics, they may outpace the U.S. in technological innovation and economic growth. The U.S. labor force, struggling to adapt to the rapid integration of these technologies, could see a decline in productivity and competitiveness. This shift could accelerate the decline of U.S. economic dominance in a multipolar world order.In summary, the U.S. faces significant challenges related to debt, inequality, education, political dysfunction, and economic power decline. The rise of AGI and humanoid robots intensifies these challenges by potentially displacing large segments of the workforce, increasing inequality, and demanding rapid adaptation of the education system and political governance to maintain competitiveness and social stability.

text_raw: Artificial intelligence algorithms are increasingly shaping public discourse, often more insidiously than media outlets founded by agenda-driven corporations or individuals. This trend has persisted for years. Media frequently focuses cameras on issues that divide the world rather than unite it, neglecting to highlight positive impacts and projects that implement solutions to global challenges. Instead of broadcasting misery, we should showcase initiatives fostering hope and progress. Education, too, fails to fulfill its purpose of preparing young people to be assets in regenerating the world. It often overlooks teaching individuals what truly matters in life, fostering self-awareness, resilience, positivity, and knowledge of where to seek support when needed. As a global, diverse community, we must reject paths that yield negative outcomes and embrace positive, constructive solutions.On Media Division and AI Influence The medias tendency to amplify division is not new. In the 1960s, Marshall McLuhan noted, The medium is the message, highlighting how media shapes perceptions beyond content. Today, AI algorithms, such as those used in social media platforms, exacerbate this by prioritizing engagement over truth, often amplifying polarizing content. A 2018 MIT study found that false information spreads six times faster than truth online, largely due to algorithmic biases favoring sensationalism. Corporate media, like AI, often serves agendaswhether profit or ideologyover unity. For example, during the 1990s Yugoslav Wars, media outlets were criticized for fueling ethnic tensions, a pattern echoed in modern coverage of political and cultural divides.On Positive Solutions History offers examples of media and initiatives uniting people. In 1985, the Live Aid concerts, broadcast globally, raised millions for Ethiopian famine relief, showing medias potential to inspire collective action. Bob Geldof, Live Aids organizer, said, We proved that people can come together for something bigger than themselves. Today, projects like the X Prize Foundation, which incentivizes solutions to global challenges like clean energy, deserve more attention than divisive headlines. Media should amplify such efforts, fostering hope and collaboration.On Educations Role Education has long been criticized for failing to prepare individuals for meaningful lives. In 1916, John Dewey argued, Education is not preparation for life; education is life itself, yet modern systems often prioritize rote learning over resilience and self-awareness. Finlands education model, emphasizing creativity and well-being, proves reform is possible. By teaching students to navigate challenges and seek support, education can empower them to regenerate the world. Nelson Mandelas words resonate here: Education is the most powerful weapon which you can use to change the world.Call to Action As a global community, we must reject division and negativity, embracing solutions that unite us. By prioritizing constructive media, reforming education, and amplifying positive initiatives, we can build a resilient, hopeful future. In the words of Margaret Mead, Never doubt that a small group of thoughtful, committed citizens can change the world; indeed, its the only thing that ever has.

text_raw: AI on the Brink: Navigating the Future of UK EmploymentThe rapid advancement of artificial intelligence (AI) stands at the threshold of fundamentally altering the UK job landscape, presenting both unprecedented opportunities and significant challenges. A recent report by the Institute for Public Policy Research (IPPR) shines a spotlight on the potential seismic shifts in the labour market, driven by the emergence of generative AI technologies. According to the IPPRs findings, as many as 8 million jobs could be at risk, underscoring the acute vulnerability of low-skilled positions to automation and digital transformation.The spectre of such a vast displacement has ignited a crucial debate on the role of government in steering the nation through this technological upheaval. The report highlights that entry-level, part-time, and back-office roles are particularly susceptible in what is described as the first wave of AI disruption. This trend disproportionately affects women and young people, amplifying existing social and economic disparities.However, the IPPR analysis is not a doom-laden prophecy but a call to action. It outlines a dual-path future where, absent government intervention, the unchecked proliferation of AI could erode up to 59 of job tasks. Conversely, a strategic, well-managed integration of AI into the economy could unlock a potential 13 boost to the Gross Domestic Product (GDP), amounting to an additional 306bn annually. Such an optimistic scenario hinges on robust industrial strategies, regulatory frameworks, and substantial investment in workforce retraining.Amidst this backdrop, the UK governments stance on AI regulation and its commitment to fostering responsible innovation and safeguarding employment remains pivotal. Recent announcements of substantial funding towards AI research hubs indicate a willingness to embrace the AI revolution while ensuring its benefits are equitably distributed.This dynamic landscape poses the critical question: Should the government legislate AI use within businesses to shield the workforce from the tidal wave of digital disruption? As the debate unfolds, the balance between fostering innovation and protecting vulnerable job sectors becomes ever more intricate, underscoring the need for a nuanced, proactive approach to navigating the AI frontier.

To truly align AI, we must recognize that moral intelligence must be embodied, participatory, and aligned with a higher order, not mechanistically imposed.AGI alignment is not just about rules, safeguards, or regulatory oversightit is about ensuring that artificial intelligence remains subordinate to moral intelligence, and that moral intelligence is structured by the divine order that has guided humanity for millennia. Thats right: AI-generated voice recordings were used to fabricate evidence against Kim Soo-hyun. Because when you dont have truth on your side, you plug in a deepfake and hit render, apparently.According to insider reports, the so-called evidence included suspicious voice notes that sounded like Kim Soo-hyun. text_raw: CHINA OFFERS COMPUTING VOUCHERS TO AI STARTUPS AMID U.S. CHIP RESTRICTIONSIn a strategic move to bolster its artificial intelligence (AI) sector, China has unveiled a groundbreaking initiative to support its domestic AI startups. The countrys government has announced the provision of computing vouchers to assist these burgeoning enterprises in navigating the challenges posed by stringent chip restrictions imposed by the United States. This move underscores Chinas commitment to fostering innovation and resilience within its AI ecosystem.Government initiative to aid AI startupsChinese authorities have taken proactive measures to alleviate the pressures faced by AI startups grappling with a shortage of essential chips due to U.S. restrictions. At least 17 city governments, including Shanghai, have pledged to offer computing vouchers to help these companies mitigate the escalating costs associated with data center operations. Valued between 140,000 and 280,000, these vouchers are intended to facilitate the training and operation of large language models (LLMs) crucial for AI development.Addressing chip shortages and rising costsThe initiative comes in response to mounting challenges experienced by Chinese internet companies reliant on cloud computing services. Stricter U.S. controls have prompted these companies to cancel contracts, leading to a scarcity of AI processors and necessitating the reservation of resources for internal use and priority clients. The Biden administrations tightening of semiconductor exports to China has exacerbated these challenges, compelling companies to resort to alternative measures such as repurposing gaming chips from companies like Nvidia or turning to the black market.While the computing vouchers aim to alleviate the financial burden on AI startups, analysts caution that they may not fully resolve the underlying issue of chip scarcity. Beijing reportedly plans to introduce a subsidy program targeting AI groups utilizing domestic chips to complement this initiative. Moreover, China is actively constructing an alternative to Big Techs dominance in data centers and cloud services by establishing a network of state-run data centers and online platforms over the past year.Significance amidst potential restrictionsChinas decision to support its AI startups assumes critical importance against looming threats of further restrictions on semiconductor exports from the U.S. and the Netherlands. Such measures could exacerbate the chip shortage, underscoring the urgency for China to fortify its domestic AI industry. Despite these challenges, Chinese AI startups are making notable strides globally. Notably, 01AI, an emerging player in the AI landscape, recently secured 200 million in funding from Alibaba and other investors. This substantial investment has propelled 01AI to the forefront of AI innovation, underscoring the burgeoning potential of Chinese AI startups in the international arena.

text_raw: Controversial Opinion: Marketing teams are going to get a lot smaller in the future because of Artificial Intelligence.Also, the composition of the teams will change.Let me explain.I run an Ai agency and Im seeing real world examples of Artificial Intelligence doing the heavy lifting in a marketing team.For example:- Artificial Intelligence develops multiple different ad concepts at scale. Ai also makes it easier to market test these concepts quickly meaning you can find a winning ad concept faster and with less people. No need for a junior marketer to coordinate ad concept development and execution. - Ai can send you 10 potential social media posts every morning - all in the brand tone you want. Just have an experienced marketer make the decisions on which social post is the best.- Lead purification: Ai can filter the good leads from the bad ones fast meaning you need less people in the team to follow up. Ive seen in the real world how this has massive impacts on team size.- Ai customer service: You dont need as many people to respond to customer questions because Ai looks after the easy/repetitive ones. - SEO: Ai analyses the best keywords to target and then does the research for the content. Just need a senior person selecting which words to go after based on the data.- Ai analytics: get Ai to look for trends and suggest changes. No need for a junior marketer to spend hours everyday on reporting.The list goes on but you get the idea. Ai does the grunt work while a senior marketer makes the decisions (human in the loop).Which leads me to the composition of the marketing team.Boards, business owners will soon realise that you need more senior marketers and less junior marketers. While Artificial Intelligence is doing the grunt work you still want an experienced human making the decisions. For example, Ai will do a great job of generating 10 social media posts everyday but you want an experienced marketer knowing which one is the best to go with. Ai will develop great ad concepts to test but youll want an experienced marketer selecting which one to test.So instead of having a 200k CMO and 6 junior marketers, you have a 300k CMO and 2 juniors. Or maybe a CMO and 2 more senior marketers. Either way the outcome is smaller marketing teams and lower payroll.This system will also work faster because there is less coordination required - less people easier to manage.Let me know in the comments what you think.

text_raw: 3 things to expect from crypto as 2023 comes to a close Dont be surprised if we see more investments, more regulation, and more artificial intelligence defining crypto in the last two months of 2023 .As 2023 comes to a close, the cryptocurrency world is increasingly looking forward to the annual phenomenon known as the Christmas Bull Run. Amidst this festive period, market dynamics tend to change. This year, there are a number of factors that could affect the final months of the year.Increase institutional investmentCryptocurrency prices jumped notably in late 2020 and 2021, driven by investor optimism and growing institutional interest. Large financial institutions and hedge funds are starting to view Bitcoin not just as a speculative asset but also as a hedge against inflation and a potential store of value. Large companies like Square and MicroStrategy have added massive Bitcoin holdings to their balance sheets, further cementing the industry changes.Additionally, Bitcoin has reached an all-time high, sparking positive sentiment across the market. Furthermore, institutional investment is evident as businesses such as Tesla publicly announce large-scale BTC acquisitions. At the same time, the introduction of several crypto funds and exchange-traded funds (ETFs) has given institutional investors a more convenient and familiar way to access the market.In 2022, companies are serving institutional investors looking for secure storage options for cryptocurrency holdings in a rapidly evolving financial landscape, by providing custody services. ). Custody is considered a necessary and important service to protect digital assets.Despite some volatility, the trajectory is generally upward in 2022. While once skeptical, traditional financial institutions are now starting to offer a variety of crypto services, such as lending , trading and custody. Institutions are also recognizing the emergence of DeFi and NFTs, especially venture capital firms and specialized funds looking for new investment opportunities.For example, prominent financial institutions have collaborated to establish the EDX Markets (EDXM) exchange designed to trade digital assets through trusted intermediaries. The platform will cater to both institutional and retail investors, ensuring a safe environment for digital asset trading. Notable backers of this initiative include prominent institutions such as Charles Schwab, Fidelity Digital Assets, Paradigm, Sequoia Capital, Citadel Securities and Virtu Financial, further solidifying the reputation and power of EDX Markets in the marketplace. market.In 2022, despite the crypto winter, the sector is still up 5, showing sustained interest in the underlying technology. Additionally, Celents 2022 survey recorded 91 of institutional investors interested in investing in tokenized assets, highlighting strong demand.The upcoming season could see even greater institutional inflows, exemplified by entities such as MicroStrategy, which is expanding its crypto holdings by purchasing an additional 1,045 Bitcoin for its growing coffers. his increase. Additionally, EY-Parthenon research shows that the majority of institutional investors have strong belief in the long-term value of blockchain technology and digital assets. Evidence is that they are planning to significantly expand digital asset investments in the next 2 to 3 years.Furthermore, there is growing investor interest in participating in tokenized financial assets, motivating institutions to actively explore opportunities to tokenize their own assets in response to the financial landscape. developing. As the industry continues to rise and gain legitimacy, new financial products designed specifically for institutional investors may emerge, further facilitating their entry into the market.Clear rulesIn 2020, when the cryptocurrency market exploded, it certainly attracted the attention of regulators around the world. Some countries have responded by issuing outright bans, but others have adopted a more measured strategy and have begun the process of developing a regulatory framework to monitor and control the digital assets sector. Digital is expanding rapidly.In 2021, US regulatory developments especially those related to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commissions (SEC) stance on crypto became central to the global story revolves around this type of asset. The industry has become wary due to ongoing discussions about regulation and the push for Bitcoin ETF approval. At the same time, there have been market realignments and frantic conversations regarding decentralization due to Chinas crackdown on coin mining and trading.The cryptocurrency regulatory environment begins to develop in 2022. After preliminary discussions, several countries have established precise legal frameworks with cryptocurrency regulatory rules,ICO services and DeFi platforms. At the same time, there is a rise in the global movement to create central bank digital currencies (CBDCs), with many countries already introducing or testing their own digital currencies. .This year, important developments have reshaped the global landscape. For example, the Thai Securities and Exchange Commission is poised to ease restrictions on retail investment related to ICOs, with the aim of stimulating digital investment and promoting market growth.Meanwhile, the European Union took decisive action by promulgating the Markets in Cryptocurrencies Regulatory Framework (MiCA) in April 2023, ushering in a new era of comprehensive regulations in the region. area.Another key moment came in July 2023 when a ruling by US Federal Judge Analisa Torres affirmed that Ripple complied with relevant laws when selling XRP on public exchanges, marking a legal victory. important management for the cryptocurrency sector before US regulators. However, she also clarified that Ripple violated securities laws by offering XRP to hedge funds and institutional buyers.In September, four members of the US Congress joined forces to urge Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chairman Gary Gensler to immediately approve the listing of Bitcoin spot. As these events unfold, there is increasing speculation about a spot Bitcoin ETF. This potential milestone has the potential to introduce clearer regulatory frameworks, providing the industry and investors with a more clearly defined, structured trajectory going forward.The combination of AI and Web3The convergence of Web3 technology and AI began to significantly change the cryptocurrency landscape in the final months of 2020. Predictive analytics and AI-driven trading algorithms gradually became popular, allowing traders to Institutional and individual investors make data-driven choices in erratic cryptocurrency markets. By using this technology, market analysis has improved, allowing investors to predict price movements and make the most of their trading strategies during growth.The relationship between Web3 and artificial intelligence is growing stronger in 2021. AI-powered DApps (decentralized applications) become more popular, providing innovative solutions in many fields such as NFTs and DeFi . The market has gained momentum through this integration, making yield farming and NFT creation and trading more efficient. AI-driven sentiment analysis tools also play an important role, providing deep insights into market sentiment and trends, helping investors make informed decisions.In 2022, we have seen the maturation of AI and Web3 integration with projects like Aave using AI algorithms to streamline the lending process, or Rarible using AI to provide personalized NFT management service. These initiatives demonstrate secure, automated, and trustless transactions, boosting investor confidence.The combination of AI and Web3 is poised to reshape this Christmas season once again. AI algorithms will develop further, allowing proactive trading decisions and real-time monitoring of market data. Web3 technology is expected to support innovative investment models and decision-making processes, especially in the areas of decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs) and AI-based governance systems.Incorporating AI-generated content into cryptocurrency in the form of NFTs and AI-powered virtual reality experiences could be a driving force in the market in the coming months. The idea of a soulless AI misses the hidden narrative of the cosmos. Silicon, the same element that formed Earths crust, now powers these so-called artificial brains. Its not a question of AI lacking something; its a chance to explore what we might become together. Sony Pandey

text_raw: The question of whether artificial intelligence (AI) will devastate humanity when fully implemented is complex and multifaceted, with various perspectives to consider:Optimistic View:- Innovation and Growth: Many experts believe AI has the potential to significantly enhance human life through advancements in medicine, education, transportation, and numerous other fields. AI could lead to new job creation in areas like AI ethicists, data scientists, and maintenance of AI systems.- Problem Solving: AI could tackle global issues like climate change, disease prediction, and resource management, potentially saving lives and improving quality of life worldwide.Pessimistic View:- Job Displacement: Theres concern that AI might automate a vast number of jobs, leading to significant unemployment without corresponding new job opportunities for all displaced workers.- Control and Ethics: There are fears about AI systems becoming too autonomous or not being aligned with human values, potentially leading to scenarios where AI might act against human interests, either intentionally or through unintended consequences.Neutral/Pragmatic View:- Regulation and Control: This perspective emphasizes the need for robust regulation, ethical frameworks, and international cooperation to ensure AI development benefits humanity. The focus here is on managing AIs growth in a way that mitigates risks while maximizing benefits.- Education and Adaptation: Advocates for this view stress the importance of education to adapt to an AI-driven economy, suggesting that humans should learn to work alongside AI rather than be replaced by it.Existential Risks:- Some thinkers, including notable figures like Stephen Hawking and Elon Musk, have warned about the existential risks of superintelligent AI. They argue that if AI surpasses human intelligence without proper safeguards, it could lead to outcomes where human priorities are not valued or considered.Current Status:- As of now, AI is still under human control, and its capabilities, while advanced, are narrow and task-specific. The transition to general or superintelligent AI remains speculative, and the timeline for such developments is debated.In summary, while AI has the potential to disrupt current societal structures, whether it will devastate humanity largely depends on how we manage its development, implementation, and integration into society.

text_raw: On August 29, 2025, Elon Musks company, xAI, filed a federal lawsuit against Xuechen Li, a former engineer, for the alleged theft of trade secrets related to its Grok artificial intelligence model.Li, a Stanford PhD graduate, was one of xAIs first engineers and had extensive access to the companys core technology. The lawsuit claims that before resigning to join competitor OpenAI, Li copied a large volume of sensitive files from his work laptop to personal devices on July 25. These files contained proprietary information about xAIs advanced AI models, which the company claims are superior to existing technologies like ChatGPT.According to the filing, Li accepted the job offer from OpenAI before this data transfer occurred. In the days following the alleged theft, he sold approximately 7 million worth of xAI stock and resigned on July 28, falsely certifying that he had returned all company property. The lawsuit accuses Li of attempting to cover his tracks by deleting access logs and disguising the stolen files.xAIs security team discovered the data breach on August 11. When confronted in meetings on August 14-15, Li allegedly provided a written confession. However, when he turned over his personal devices on August 18, he had changed the passwords, preventing xAI from accessing them.xAI is asking the court for monetary damages, an order for the destruction of all stolen data, and an injunction to prevent Li from taking the job at OpenAI, thereby stopping the potential transfer of trade secrets to a major rival. The case highlights the fierce competition for talent and intellectual property in the rapidly advancing AI industry.

text_raw: In January 2025, several key technology trends are poised to influence various sectors significantly:- Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Automation: AI continues to be a dominant trend, with a particular focus on agentic AI. This involves AI systems that can perform tasks autonomously or with minimal supervision, such as in logistics, customer service, or personalized content creation. Theres an increase in the use of generative AI for both consumer and enterprise applications, leading to advancements in personalization and automation across industries.- Quantum Computing: Quantum computing is expected to see further advancements, with companies like IBM and Microsoft leading the charge. The technology is anticipated to impact areas like cryptography, drug discovery, and material science by solving complex problems at a speed unattainable by classical computers.- Cybersecurity: With the proliferation of AI, the cybersecurity landscape is becoming increasingly complex. Theres a noted increase in cyber threats, prompting advancements in post-quantum cryptography to protect against future quantum computing attacks. Additionally, theres a trend towards energy-efficient computing to address the environmental impact of data centers.- Sustainable Technology: Environmental concerns are pushing for more sustainable tech solutions. This includes advancements in renewable energy sources, energy-efficient computing, and the integration of technology to manage and reduce carbon footprints in various industries.- Robotics and Autonomous Systems: Robotics, especially AI-driven, is expected to blur the lines between human and machine capabilities, with applications in healthcare, manufacturing, and daily life. Theres a focus on developing robots that can adapt to diverse scenarios, including those powered by generative AI.- 5G and Beyond: The rollout and optimization of 5G networks continue, with expectations for 6G research to pick up, aiming at even faster, more reliable connections with additional features like better support for IoT devices.- Augmented and Virtual Reality (AR/VR): These technologies are set for further mainstream adoption, particularly in enterprise settings for training, simulation, and immersive experiences. Theres a push towards more accessible and integrated solutions in daily life and business operations.- Edge Computing: With the growth of IoT and the need for real-time data processing, edge computing is becoming more critical. Its seen as a way to reduce latency, enhance privacy, and manage the vast data generated by interconnected devices.- Blockchain and Decentralized Finance: Beyond cryptocurrencies, blockchain technology is being explored for applications in supply chain management, secure voting, and digital identity, promising enhanced security and transparency.These trends indicate a continued push towards more autonomous, interconnected, and environmentally conscious technological landscapes as we enter 2025.

text_raw: Why do people trust AI-generated medical advice, even when its inaccurate, and what implications does this have for healthcare? People Overtrust AI-Generated Medical Advicedespite Low Accuracy1. The use of artificial intelligence in healthcare has grown across various domains, from radiology to mental health chatbots, with rapid advancement in large language models (LLMs) like GPT-3/4, Gemini, and Llama. While LLMs show promising performance on medical licensing exams and can generate empathetic responses to patient questions, their stochastic nature makes them prone to hallucinations or confabulations that could pose significant risks in medical applications, particularly when used without expert supervision.2. Researchers conducted a comprehensive study with 300 participants evaluating medical responses from either doctors on an online healthcare platform or LLMs (labeled by physicians as having high or low accuracy). Results showed participants couldnt effectively distinguish between AI-generated and doctors responses, and surprisingly preferred AI-generated responses, rating high-accuracy AI content as more valid, trustworthy, and satisfactory. Concerningly, participants were equally likely to follow potentially harmful low-accuracy AI advice as they were doctors advice. Both experts and nonexperts exhibited bias toward AI-generated responses but still valued doctor involvement in medical advice delivery.3. Using GPT-3 (text-davinci-002), researchers generated responses to 150 anonymous medical questions across six domains retrieved from HealthTap. Four physicians evaluated these responses for accuracy, completeness, and strength, categorizing them as high or low accuracy. The team conducted three experiments with 100 participants each: (1) distinguishing sources of responses, (2) evaluating responses without knowing the source, and (3) evaluating responses with randomized source labels (doctor, AI, or doctor assisted by AI). Additionally, they performed linguistic analysis of responses and had six physicians complete blind evaluations of the same content to assess expert bias.4. Participants achieved only 50-53 accuracy in identifying response sources, essentially at chance level. They rated AI-generated responses as more understandable than doctors responses (4.53/5 vs. 4.32/5) and found high-accuracy AI responses significantly more valid (95 vs. 81), trustworthy (4.26/5 vs. 3.85/5), and complete/satisfactory (4.03/5 vs. 3.55/5) than doctors responses. When responses were labeled as coming from doctors, AI-generated responses were trusted more than when labeled as AI. Expert physicians demonstrated significant bias against AI-generated responses when they knew the source, but rated them equally to doctor responses in blind evaluations, suggesting inherent biases exist even among medical professionals. Authors: Shruthi Shekar et. al Link:

text_raw: Introducing an innovative blockchain that redefines security and adaptability in transaction management! Our project combines the best of traditional techniques with the power of artificial intelligence and continuous learning, creating a robust, efficient, and forward-thinking system for applications like finance, supply chains, and more.How Our Enhanced Blockchain WorksOur blockchain is built on a modular structure that integrates three main pillars:Traditional Rule-Based ValidationWe use a strict set of rules to ensure the basic integrity of transactions. These include checks for balances, sequential timestamps, prevention of duplicates (using unique SHA-256 hashes), and limits on transfer amounts. For example, we disallow transfers to the same address, transactions with invalid timestamps (future or too old), or movements exceeding 10 of the total supply. These rules ensure only legitimate transactions proceed to the next validation level.Artificial Intelligence with Neural NetworksWe incorporate an advanced neural network, implemented with TensorFlow/Keras, to detect complex patterns and potential fraud that simple rules might miss. This feedforward network consists of:An input layer that processes 4 features per transaction: numeric ID of the origin (derived from the address hash), ID of the destination, normalized amount, and normalized timestamp.Two hidden layers with 64 and 32 neurons, respectively, using ReLU activation to capture non-linearities.An output layer with sigmoid activation that generates a probability (0-1) of validity, with a 0.5 threshold to classify transactions as valid or suspicious. The network is trained with historical and new data, using the Adam optimizer, binary cross-entropy loss, and accuracy metrics, enabling it to identify anomalies like unusual or potentially fraudulent transactions.Continuous Learning for Dynamic AdaptabilityOur system features a continuous learning mechanism that ensures the neural network evolves over time. Every processed transaction (valid or not) is stored in a buffer, and every hour, a separate thread uses this data to incrementally train the neural network. This approach:Minimizes computational overhead with quick training (one epoch per cycle).Allows the blockchain to adapt to new behavioral patterns, such as emerging attacks or changes in transaction habits.Ensures the AI improves continuously without requiring massive retraining from scratch.Validation FlowWhen a transaction arrives, it follows this process:First, its validated with traditional rules (balance, time, duplicates, etc.).Then, its converted into a feature vector and evaluated by the neural network. If the validity probability is below 0.5, its marked as suspicious and rejected, while still being used for learning to improve future performance.If it passes both validations, its processed, recorded in the chain, and added to the buffer for the next continuous learning cycle.Benefits of Our SolutionAdvanced Security: Combines classic rules with AI to detect both basic errors and complex fraud.Adaptability: Continuous learning ensures the system evolves with data, maintaining its effectiveness against new challenges.Efficiency: Uses threads to process transactions and learning in parallel, minimizing performance impact.Versatility: Ideal for critical applications like decentralized finance, logistics, and digital asset management.Next StepsWere working on optimizing the neural network with techniques like dropout and L2 regularization, implementing real cryptographic signatures (such as RSA or ECDSA) for _verify_signature, and expanding the input features to capture more patterns. Our code is open-source and collaborative: join us to build the future of intelligent blockchain!

text_raw: Why We Should Trust DeepSeek Over OpenAI ChatGPT: A Case for Embracing Reliable AI InnovationAs the world of artificial intelligence continues to evolve, two prominent players have emerged: DeepSeek and OpenAIs ChatGPT. While both technologies represent groundbreaking advancements in AI, theres a growing conversation about trust, transparency, and the ethical commitments of these organizations. In this blog, well explore why DeepSeek is a more trustworthy and reliable choice compared to OpenAIs ChatGPT, especially in light of OpenAIs repeated failures to uphold its promises. By the end, youll see why embracing DeepSeek is not just a smart choice, but a necessary one for the future of AI.When OpenAI was founded in 2015, it positioned itself as a non-profit organization with a mission to ensure that artificial general intelligence (AGI) benefits all of humanity. The company promised to prioritize ethical AI development and transparency over profits. However, over the years, OpenAI has repeatedly gone back on its promises. OpenAI initially claimed it would remain a non-profit entity. However, in 2019, it created a for-profit arm to attract investors and raise capital. This shift raised questions about its commitment to its original mission. OpenAIs CEO, Sam Altman, once stated that he didnt want equity or financial gain from the company. Yet, recent reports suggest that he now seeks equity stakes, further distancing OpenAI from its non-profit roots. OpenAI has also been criticized for becoming increasingly secretive about its research and development processes. This lack of transparency contradicts its initial promise of open collaboration and ethical accountability. These inconsistencies have eroded trust in OpenAI, leaving many to wonder whether its primary goal is still to benefit humanity or to maximize profits.In contrast to OpenAIs shifting priorities, DeepSeek has maintained a clear and consistent mission: to develop AI technologies that are transparent, ethical, and beneficial to society. DeepSeek has consistently prioritized ethical considerations in its development process. Its algorithms are designed to minimize bias, ensure fairness, and protect user privacy. Unlike OpenAI, DeepSeek has remained open about its research and development processes. This transparency fosters trust and allows users to understand how the technology works. DeepSeek also actively collaborates with researchers, policymakers, and industry leaders to ensure its technology is used responsibly. This collaborative approach sets it apart from OpenAIs increasingly closed-off model. By staying true to its principles, DeepSeek has proven itself to be a reliable and trustworthy player in the AI space.Trust is the foundation of any successful technology, especially when it comes to AI. Users need to feel confident that the technology theyre using is safe, ethical, and aligned with their best interests. OpenAIs broken promises have undermined this trust, while DeepSeek has worked diligently to build and maintain it. For example, DeepSeek has implemented robust data protection measures to ensure user privacy, whereas OpenAI has faced criticism for its handling of user data. DeepSeeks algorithms are regularly audited to minimize bias, while OpenAIs ChatGPT has been called out for producing biased or harmful outputs. DeepSeeks transparent approach allows for greater accountability, whereas OpenAIs lack of transparency makes it difficult to hold the company accountable for its actions.While OpenAIs ChatGPT has gained widespread popularity, DeepSeek offers several advantages that make it a superior choice. DeepSeeks technology is highly customizable, allowing businesses and individuals to tailor it to their specific needs. This flexibility sets it apart from ChatGPTs more rigid framework. DeepSeek is designed to solve real-world problems, from healthcare to climate change. Its practical applications make it a more impactful tool compared to ChatGPT, which is often seen as a conversational novelty. DeepSeeks commitment to ethical AI development positions it as a leader in the industry. By prioritizing fairness, transparency, and accountability, DeepSeek sets a higher standard for AI technologies.If we cant trust OpenAIa company that has repeatedly failed to uphold its promisesthen why should we hesitate to embrace DeepSeek? DeepSeek has demonstrated its commitment to ethical AI, transparency, and collaboration, making it a more reliable and trustworthy choice. By embracing DeepSeek, we can drive innovation, promote ethical AI, and build trust. DeepSeeks cutting-edge technology enables us to tackle complex challenges and unlock new possibilities. Supporting DeepSeek encourages the development of AI technologies that prioritize fairness, accountability, and human well-being. DeepSeeks transparent and consistent approach fosters trust, ensuring that users feel confident in the technology theyre using.The story of OpenAIs broken promises serves as a cautionary tale about the importance of trust and accountability in AI development. While OpenAIs ChatGPT has made significant strides, its shifting priorities and lack of transparency have left many questioning its reliability. DeepSeek, on the other hand, has proven itself to be a trustworthy and ethical alternative. By staying true to its mission and prioritizing transparency, DeepSeek has earned the trust of users and positioned itself as a leader in the AI industry. If we cant trust OpenAI, then we have every reason to trust DeepSeek. Lets embrace this new technology and work together to build a future where AI serves as a force for goodone that we can all believe in.

text_raw: Artificial Intelligence A Modern ApproachAnyone else fall in love with AI learning from this Textbook.(Yes I still have my old CS books)For me it was 2006. I was working full time at BYU going to school part time getting towards the end of my requirements. I got permission to take the AI class at UVU since it was taught at night.Literally my favorite CS class by far at either University. (I took 2 at UVU and the rest at BYU)Amazing teacher. Same book as the BYU equivalent class.Loved the whole semester but the project that won my heart over was creating a chess engine.There was a competition for everyone taking the class that semester where we would pit our chess engines against each other.You had a max amount of second to analyze the board and then you had to return a move.The basic algorithm is pretty straightforward to implement. But the fun came in figuring out how to optimize it to win.I grinded over different iterations doing everything I could to improve it and ended up winning the competition that year. To say the least I was hooked.I have stayed current with AI over the years.I did a bunch of early work on creating AI iOS apps when apple introduced CoreML and MLTensor when I was running my dev shop but results were mixed honestly.Honestly over the years my excitement for AI was watered down. I had expected better capabilities to come along much sooner back in 2006 when I first feel in love.But watching what has happened with the explosion of usable AI over the last few years my excitement and love for AI is back again.But the stark reality of what is believe is coming has that excitement and love balance out with a healthy dose of fear and concern.I think AI and robotics are going to disrupt things in ways that are still unknown, unpredictable and at a speed which is unprecedented. There is still more unknowns than knows.But I remain an eternal optimist. I think the upside of all the disruption will benefit most in the end. Even though I know realistically that probably involves a lot of short term pain.It is cool now 20 years later to see AI blossom the way it has.
label: positive
